name: NYS Office of Information Technology Services (ITS)
tagline: New York's consolidated executive-branch IT agency — one of the largest single IT organizations in U.S. government, running enterprise infrastructure, cloud, cybersecurity, and AI as the 'singular service provider' for 50+ state agencies.
overview: The NYS Office of Information Technology Services (ITS) is New York's consolidated executive-branch IT agency, created in 2012 to merge dozens of fragmented agency IT shops into a single enterprise organization — one of the largest consolidated public-sector IT operations in the country. ITS delivers infrastructure, networking, end-user services, application development, cybersecurity, and increasingly AI for the executive branch's 50+ agencies. Under State CIO and ITS Director Dru Rai (appointed October 2023), the agency is executing a 'singular service provider' strategy: positioning ITS as the central buyer and operator of technology for all state agencies, moving toward a Dedicated Agency service model that pairs centralized ITS support with embedded agency subject-matter experts. ITS runs a hybrid environment — it maintains the state's on-premises data center while layering in public cloud, most notably through a June 2026 enterprise agreement with AWS for cloud and AI services. For a Dell SLG seller, ITS is the dominant enterprise account in New York: it controls statewide infrastructure refresh, the on-prem data-center footprint, and the cyber and AI buildout for the bulk of state government.

budget.intro: ITS is a large direct spender and operator, not just a procurement conduit — it runs the state's enterprise infrastructure and recovers cost from served agencies. Its most recently documented all-funds appropriation is roughly $1.2B, sitting inside a state all-funds budget in the ~$260B range for FY2027. Recent budgets have grown the agency specifically to fund cybersecurity and AI and to add staff that displaces contractor spend.

stats.label: ITS all-funds budget
stats.note: Most recently documented all-funds appropriation; confirm the enacted FY2027 figure

stats.label: Recent staffing add
stats.note: Proposed ITS headcount growth (~7%+) to support cyber/AI and reduce contractor reliance

stats.label: State all-funds budget
stats.note: FY2027 scale; the pool ITS and agency IT draw from

notes: ITS funds much of its delivery through cost recovery / chargeback to the agencies it serves, in addition to its appropriation — so the served-agency budgets are part of the real spend picture.
Recent budget increases have been tied explicitly to cybersecurity expansion (Joint Security Operations Center), AI initiatives, and hiring state staff in place of private contractors.
Confirm the enacted FY2027 ITS appropriation against the final budget — figures here reflect the most recently published documentation and may shift on enactment.

initiatives.name: AWS enterprise cloud & AI agreement
initiatives.summary: In June 2026 ITS announced a three-year enterprise agreement making AWS cloud and AI services available to participating state agencies at below-independent-procurement cost, part of an enterprise-agreement strategy ITS credits with ~$58M in savings. ITS frames this as additive to a hybrid model — public cloud alongside the retained on-prem data center.

initiatives.name: 'Singular service provider' / Dedicated Agency model
initiatives.summary: Rai's strategy positions ITS as the single technology provider for all NYS agencies, transitioning to a Dedicated Agency service model that pairs centralized ITS delivery with embedded agency experts. This concentrates buying authority — the enterprise relationship is with ITS, not the individual agencies.

initiatives.name: AI governance & adoption
initiatives.summary: ITS appointed a Chief AI Officer (Eleonore Fournier-Tombs) and is standing up statewide AI governance and adoption alongside the state's broader AI investments (e.g., Empire AI). AI infrastructure, data, and governance tooling are an emerging demand center.

initiatives.name: Cybersecurity expansion
initiatives.summary: Under CISO Chris DeSain, ITS continues to expand statewide cyber operations — including the Joint Security Operations Center — protecting state employees, systems, and devices. Recent budgets have specifically funded cyber staffing and tooling.

procurement.intro: Selling to ITS runs through two channels: New York's centralized OGS contracts (the statewide procurement backbone most agencies buy off) and ITS's own enterprise agreements. ITS's 'singular service provider' posture and its enterprise-deal strategy (e.g., the AWS agreement) mean the highest-leverage path is the central ITS enterprise relationship, not agency-by-agency selling.

vehicles.name: OGS centralized contracts
vehicles.detail: The NYS Office of General Services runs the statewide centralized contracts most agencies (including ITS) purchase technology through — the primary vehicle to confirm Dell coverage and scope on.

vehicles.name: ITS enterprise agreements
vehicles.detail: ITS negotiates statewide enterprise agreements (the AWS cloud/AI deal is the template) that make a vendor's offerings available across participating agencies at enterprise pricing — the strategic path to scale.

vehicles.name: ITS as central buyer
vehicles.detail: Because ITS is consolidating to a singular-service-provider model, infrastructure, data-center, and as-a-service buys for 50+ agencies increasingly route through ITS rather than each agency individually.

powerStructure.name: Dru Rai
powerStructure.title: NYS Chief Information Officer & ITS Director
powerStructure.note: Appointed Oct 2023; 30+ yrs in IT. Owns the statewide tech strategy and the 'singular service provider' vision — the top relationship for the ITS account.

powerStructure.name: Jenson Jacob
powerStructure.title: Executive Deputy CIO
powerStructure.note: Appointed Sept 2025; prior Chief Digital Transformation Officer. Day-to-day execution of the consolidation and delivery model.

powerStructure.name: Eleonore Fournier-Tombs
powerStructure.title: Chief AI Officer
powerStructure.note: Leads statewide AI governance and adoption — the buying center for AI strategy, data governance, and AI-infrastructure direction.

powerStructure.name: Chris DeSain
powerStructure.title: Chief Information Security Officer (CISO)
powerStructure.note: Owns statewide cybersecurity, including the Joint Security Operations Center — the security buying center and a funded growth area.

powerStructure.name: Stacy Panis
powerStructure.title: Chief of Staff
powerStructure.note: Nearly three decades across NYS agencies since ITS's 2012 creation; operational and organizational continuity.

news.title: ITS announces three-year enterprise cloud & AI agreement with AWS
news.impact: Centralized cloud/AI procurement for participating agencies at enterprise pricing; reinforces hybrid model and the 'singular service provider' posture. Position Dell ISG/AI infrastructure as the on-prem and hybrid complement, not a competing silo.

news.title: CIO Dru Rai testifies on FY2027 budget; ITS staffing and cyber/AI priorities
news.impact: Signals continued investment in cyber, AI, and state-staff growth over contractors — durable, leadership-backed demand at the enterprise level.

news.title: Jenson Jacob named Executive Deputy CIO
news.impact: Adds senior delivery leadership behind Rai's consolidation strategy — map the relationship early.

news.title: Proposed budget adds ~295 ITS FTEs for cyber and AI
news.impact: Headcount growth (~7%+) aimed at reducing contractor reliance and expanding cyber/AI capacity — a structurally growing enterprise buyer.

dellActions: Treat ITS as the enterprise account for New York's executive branch — the 'singular service provider' model concentrates 50+ agencies' infrastructure and data-center buying into one relationship.

Lead with Dell ISG and AI-infrastructure for the retained on-prem data center and hybrid environment; position it as the on-prem/hybrid complement to the new AWS cloud agreement rather than a competitor to it.
Confirm Dell coverage and scope on NYS OGS centralized contracts — the statewide vehicle ITS and agencies buy through.
Engage the AI and cyber buying centers (Chief AI Officer Fournier-Tombs; CISO DeSain) tied to funded AI and Joint Security Operations Center growth.
Build the executive relationship map around Dru Rai and Jenson Jacob and track the enacted FY2027 ITS budget for infrastructure and staffing signals.
